content:
Systems Specification: Idempotent Lifecycle Automation

Goal: enforce deterministic Linux system state via POSIX-compliant, idempotent Bash automation with strict-mode failure semantics.

1. Engineering Standards (Strict Mode + POSIX)

Automation is production-grade only when failure behavior is deterministic. Every script enforces Strict Mode Engineering: set -euo pipefail to fail fast on errors, prevent unbound variables, and preserve predictable execution paths.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\t\n'

POSIX compliance requirement: all logic is implemented with portable constructs so scripts behave consistently across RHEL and Ubuntu baselines.

2. Idempotency Logic (Check-Then-Execute)

User provisioning and filesystem configuration are implemented as convergent operations. The scripts check desired state before mutating the system, guaranteeing that repeated runs do not duplicate users, re-apply permissions incorrectly, or introduce configuration drift.

CHECK-THEN-EXECUTE

if user_exists(desired_uid_gid) then
  ensure_shell_and_groups(&update idempotently)
else
  generate_entropy_credential()
  create_user(uid_gid)
  seed /etc/skel with desired baseline
fi

Golden Image requirement: enforce consistency of UIDs, GIDs, and /etc/skel deployment outputs across multiple provisioning cycles.

3. Hardening Profile (sysctl + fstab)

Hardening is applied automatically at provisioning time to reduce runtime attack surface.

Kernel Network Security via sysctl

Automates tuning of /etc/sysctl.conf including IP spoofing protections and relevant network stack safeguards aligned with CIS Linux Benchmark targets.

Mount Option Enforcement via /etc/fstab

Configures mount flags such as noexec and nosuid to prevent execution and privilege escalation on non-trusted mount points.

4. Challenge (Input Validation & Sanitization)

Shell automation is frequently exposed to injection risk through unexpected environment variables or operator-supplied values. Mitigation includes local scoping for variables, strict quoting, and sanitization gates before any privileged action.

5. Audit Trail (SIEM Ingestion)

All automated actions are piped to logger with structured prefixes so centralized monitoring can ingest events for security auditing and operational troubleshooting.

logger -t 'linux-admin-scripting' -- "action=create_user uid=... gid=... result=..."

6. Standards Mapping

Compliance anchors include NIST-aligned access control and system integrity practices, and CIS Benchmarks for baseline hardening. Deterministic automation reduces configuration drift to support audit-ready operations.
